# The Creative Learning Framework

The 4 P's: Projects, Passion, Peers, Play.

**Case: Scratch. 50 million children have created projects. Low floor, high ceiling, wide walls.**

**Case: The Computer Clubhouse. After-school creative learning centers in underserved communities.**

**Case: The Learning Spiral. Imagine, Create, Play, Share, Reflect — the cycle of creative learning.**

**Case: The Kindergarten Model. Children create with blocks, paint, and stories. This should continue.**

**Case: The Foreword. Sir Ken Robinson endorses Resnick's approach.**
---

*Generated by [Heardly App](https://www.heard.ly) — turning books into knowledge you can Listen and Execute.*

**Case: The Animation. A child animates a story in Scratch. They learn sequencing, logic, and storytelling.**
**Case: The Robot. Children build and program robots. They learn engineering and coding.**
**Case: The Music. Children create music with code. They learn patterns and creativity.**
**Case: The Game. Children design and code their own games. They learn design thinking.**
**Case: The Story. Children create interactive stories. They learn narrative and interactivity.**
**Case: The Loop. The creative learning spiral repeats. Each cycle deepens understanding.**
**Case: The Block. Scratch uses visual blocks. No syntax errors. Low floor for beginners.**
**Case: The Spiral. Each cycle of imagine-create-play-share-reflect builds on the last.**
